project_type: C++ single-compilation-unit AI system with Telegram bot interface
build_system: make with clang++, single compilation unit (mk_entry.cpp includes all .cpp files)
test_framework: Custom test framework in tests/test_main.cpp which includes tests/test_integration.cpp; uses TEST_ASSERT macros, RUN_TEST runner
verification_instructions: 1. Run 'make' from MK_OS directory to compile. 2. Run 'make test' to run test suite. 3. Both must pass (currently 3 tests fail in test_thinking_to_decision_flow).
setup_instructions: The project compiles with clang++ -std=c++17, requires libcurl and libsqlite3. Build with 'make' from MK_OS directory.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Build on Linux (Makefile uses -DDARWIN flag but compiles fine). No Docker needed.
contribution_requirements: Push to main branch directly (user needs it live immediately)
key_patterns: Single compilation unit: system/mk_entry.cpp #includes all other .cpp files. All classes are header-only style in .cpp files with include guards. Telegram bot uses long-polling loop in telegram_poll_loop(). Command dispatch is in mk_entry.cpp lines 1896-1984. LLM responses are generated via generate_ai_response(). The MK_SYSTEM_PROMPT is a static const at line 806. brainMemory has max 6 turns so it's bounded.
directory_structure: MK_OS/ is the main project dir. system/ has mk_entry.cpp (main entry). plugins/ has telegram.cpp. llm/ has thinking_engine.cpp, cloud_llm.cpp, provider_router.cpp. ai_core/ has decision_engine.cpp, smart_router, conversation_mode. mk_brain/ has brain_memory. tests/ has test_main.cpp and test_integration.cpp.
